paper_title: U-Pb Zircon Age Analysis of the Traiguén Formation

methods.description: Process for determining the age of deposition and sedimentary provenance of the Traiguén Formation through U-Pb analyses on zircon grains.

steps.description: Heavy mineral concentrates of the <350 µm fraction from eight rock samples were separated using traditional techniques at ZirChron LLC (Tucson, Arizona).
steps.searchable_summary: separate heavy mineral concentrates from rock samples

steps.description: Zircons from the nonmagnetic fraction were mounted on a 1-in.-diameter (~2.5-cm-diameter) epoxy puck and then polished to expose the granular surface.

After cathodoluminescence (CL) imaging at the University of Idaho, the laser ablation-inductively coupled plasma-mass spectrometry (LA-ICP-MS) U-Pb analyses were conducted at Washington State University (Pullman, Washington) using a New Wave Nd:YAG ultraviolet 213 nm laser coupled to a Thermo Scientific Element 2 single-collector, double-focusing, magnetic sector ICP-MS.
steps.searchable_summary: perform LA-ICP-MS U-Pb analyses on zircon grains

steps.description: Time-independent fractionation was corrected by normalizing U/Pb and Pb/Pb ratios of the unknowns to the zircon standards (Chang et al., 2006).
steps.searchable_summary: normalize U/Pb and Pb/Pb ratios using zircon standards

steps.description: Concentrations of U and Th were monitored by comparison to NIST 610 trace-element glass standard.
steps.searchable_summary: monitor U and Th concentrations using NIST 610 standard

steps.description: U-Pb ages were calculated and plots were generated using Isoplot (Ludwig, 2003; Fig. 7).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate U-Pb ages and generate plots using Isoplot

steps.description: The U-Pb zircon age errors for a set of analyses are reported using the quadratic sum of the analytical error plus the total systematic error (Valencia et al., 2005).
steps.searchable_summary: report U-Pb zircon age errors using quadratic sum of errors
